Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Plntf'S Responses To Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Apr-03-2006 Text Ruling

Ntc Of Mo & Mo For Order Staying Depositions For Production Of Business Records & Quashing Ntc Of Depositions, & For Order Imposing A Monetary Sanction Against Eemax, Inc. & Its Attys Murphy, Pearson, Bradley & Feeney
Off Calendar Mar-22-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Dean Anthony Alper, appeared for Plaintiff/Moving party
Helen Candell, appeared for Defendant/Opposing party
Ruling:
Hearing held on Plaintiff Truck Insurance Exchange's Motion to Stay Depositions for Production of Business Records and Quashing Notice of Depositions and for Monetary Sanctions.
Motion is DENIED - as to subpoenas to Farmer's Insurance Group and Farmer''s Subrogation COE & GRANTED as to DPI. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Eemax, Inc. To Serve Answers To Truck Insurance Exchange'S Special Interrogs And Request For Sanctions Against Deft And Its Attorneys Of Record
Off Calendar Mar-21-2006 Text Ruling

Ntc Of Mo And Mo To Eemax, Inc. To Serve Answers To Truck Insurance Exchange'S Special Interrogs And Request For Sanctions Against Deft And Its Attorneys Of Record
Off Calendar Mar-22-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Dean Anthony Alper, appeared for Plaintiff/Moving party
Helen Candell, appeared for Defendant/Opposing party
Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Truck Insurance Exchange's Motion To Compel EEMAX, Inc. To Serve Answers To Truck Insurance Exchange's Special Interrogatories and Request For Sanctions is granted in part and denied in part.
The motion is granted as to Special Interrogatory Nos. 1, 4, and 6. The objections are overruled and ordered stricken.
The motion is granted as to Special Interrogatory Nos. 2 and 3, limited to complaints made in writing.
The motion is denied as to Special Interrogatory No. 5.
The motion is denied as to Special Interrogatory No. 7.
The motion is granted as to Special Interrogatory No. 8, limited to the SP3512.
The motion is granted as to Special Interrogatory Nos. 9 and 10. The objections are overruled and ordered stricken. Defendant is ordered to respond further. By this order, the court is not determining that Defendant is making the contentions that are the subject of the interrogatories.
Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order.
All requests for sanctions are denied.
Order signed in open court.
COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-22-2006 Set For Trial On Aug-28-06 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Aug-10-06 At 03:30 P.M. In Dept 613
